details: Beginning SFPU kernel analysis for atanh operation
SINH uses eltwise_sfpu.cpp compute kernel, SFPU_OP_CHAIN_0 expands to sinh_tile(0), init is sinh_tile_init(), macro SFPU_OP_SINH_INCLUDE=1, approx_mode=false (default case)
ATANH: compute_kernel=eltwise_sfpu.cpp, init=atanh_tile_init(), func=atanh_tile(idst), macro=SFPU_OP_ATANH_INCLUDE, approx_mode=false (default)
HARDTANH is parametrized type, but dispatch code is nuked. Need to find SFPU implementation from LLK layer.
Read ckernel_sfpu_atanh.h (WH and BH identical), llk_math_eltwise_unary_sfpu_atanh.h, API header atanh.h, params dispatch
Verifying all SFPU function names and instruction identifiers

APPROX=false for SINH. Generated as constexpr bool in genfiles.cpp:394 from math_approx_mode. get_op_approx_mode() returns false (default case). sinh_tile_init() and sinh_tile() use APPROX as template arg, which means APPROXIMATION_MODE=false in the SFPU kernel.

Core SFPU found at ckernel_sfpu_hardtanh.h. API header and LLK dispatch are nuked. Compute kernel is eltwise_sfpu.cpp. HARDTANH is parameterized type with 3 uint32 params (param0, param1, param2) in FP16_B format.
All SFPU identifiers verified: calculate_atanh, atanh_init found in both WH and BH. File paths verified. SFPI intrinsics map to: SFPLOAD, SFPSTORE, SFPMAD, SFPEXEXP, SFPSETEXP, SFPCAST

key_findings: Uses SFPI abstractions (vFloat, vInt, dst_reg, v_if). Implements sinh via exp_21f helper (Moroz 2^z algorithm) with Taylor fallback for small |x|. References undefined _float_to_int32_positive_ function.

details: All SFPU identifiers verified: _calculate_hardtanh_ exists in WH+BH, all file paths confirmed, all SFPU instruction patterns confirmed in source.

Traced hardtanh algorithm. Three additions + two conditional zeroings implement clamp. Derived correct param values: p0=-min_val, p1=min_val-max_val, p2=max_val. Note: comment for param2 says -(pos_threshold) but mathematical derivation requires pos_threshold (max_val). The param2 comment may have a sign error.

key_findings: Implements clamp via 3 SFPMAD additions and 2 v_if/v_endif conditional zeroings. WH and BH versions byte-identical. APPROXIMATION_MODE unused.
